definition: A legal document (Form 26) submitted by a candidate disclosing their educational background, assets, liabilities, and criminal record (if any). It is made public for transparency.
example: Candidates must file an affidavit to ensure voters know their history.

definition: An agreement between two or more political parties to work together, usually to contest elections or form a government.
example: Pre-poll alliances help parties pool their vote bases.

definition: A system of voting in secret. Historically done with paper, now primarily done through Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) in India.
example: The secret ballot is a pillar of Indian democracy.

definition: A local government officer who works on behalf of the ECI to maintain and update the voter list for a specific polling booth.
example: The BLO visited my house to verify my voter registration details.

definition: An election held to fill a vacancy caused by the death, resignation, or disqualification of an elected member before the end of their term.
example: A by-election was held after the MP resigned to join the state assembly.

definition: The period before an election when candidates and parties reach out to voters through rallies, speeches, and advertisements to seek their support.
example: The campaign trail across the state saw many large rallies.

definition: An individual who stands for election to represent a constituency in a legislative body like the Lok Sabha or Vidhan Sabha.
example: There were 15 candidates contesting from our constituency.

definition: A government formed by an alliance of multiple political parties when no single party wins a clear majority of seats.
example: A coalition government requires consensus among all allied parties.

definition: A specific geographical area defined for electoral purposes, which elects one representative to a legislative body.
example: Each voter belongs to one Lok Sabha and one Vidhan Sabha constituency.

definition: The process of tallying the votes recorded in EVMs and postal ballots to determine the winner of an election.
example: Counting began at 8 AM across all centers simultaneously.

definition: The Election Commission of India, an independent constitutional body responsible for conducting free and fair elections in India.
example: The ECI announced the 7-phase schedule for the general elections.

definition: The official list of all citizens who are registered and eligible to vote in a particular constituency. Also called the Voter List.
example: Is your name on the Electoral Roll for this booth?

definition: Electronic Voting Machine, a standalone device used to record and count votes electronically. It is secure and fast.
example: EVMs have simplified the voting and counting process in India.

definition: Elector's Photo Identity Card, commonly known as the Voter ID card. It serves as identity and address proof for voters.
example: I presented my EPIC at the polling station to get my finger inked.

definition: A trust vote in the legislature to prove that the government has the support of a majority of the members.
example: The Governor called for a floor test within 24 hours.

definition: A situation where no single political party or pre-poll alliance wins enough seats to form a majority on its own.
example: A hung parliament often leads to intense post-poll negotiations.

definition: Model Code of Conduct, a set of ethical guidelines for parties and candidates enforced by ECI from the date of election announcement.
example: The MCC prevents the ruling party from using government cars for rallies.

definition: A public declaration of policies and goals issued by a political party before an election to explain what they will do if elected.
example: The party's manifesto promised new infrastructure and jobs.

definition: The difference in the number of votes between the winning candidate and the runner-up.
example: The candidate won by a narrow margin of just 500 votes.

definition: Member of Legislative Assembly, a representative elected by voters to the state's Vidhan Sabha.
example: The MLA raised the issue of water shortage in the assembly.

definition: Member of Parliament, a representative elected to the Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha at the national level.
example: The MP represents our district in the Parliament in Delhi.

definition: The formal process where a person applies to be a candidate in an election by filing papers and paying a deposit.
example: Today is the last date for filing nominations for the third phase.

definition: None Of The Above, an option on the EVM for voters who do not want to vote for any of the listed candidates.
example: If you don't like any candidate, you can press the NOTA button.

definition: The parties in the legislature that are not part of the government, responsible for questioning and checking the ruling party.
example: The Opposition demanded a debate on the new bill.

definition: The location (often a school or community hall) where voters go to cast their ballots on election day.
example: My polling station is just two blocks away from my home.

definition: A government official appointed by ECI for a constituency, responsible for overseeing the entire election process there.
example: The Returning Officer declared the final results after the count.

definition: The process where the Returning Officer examines all nomination papers to ensure they are valid and legal.
example: Many nominations were rejected during the scrutiny phase.

definition: The 48-hour window before the end of polling when all active campaigning must stop by law.
example: The silent period allows voters to decide without campaign noise.

definition: The percentage of eligible voters who actually cast their vote in an election.
example: The voter turnout in the 2024 elections was record-breaking.

definition: Voter Verified Paper Audit Trail, a device that prints a paper slip confirming the vote cast on the EVM.
example: VVPAT slips are used for sample verification of EVM results.

definition: A direction issued by a political party to its members in the legislature on how to vote on a specific issue.
example: Defying the party whip can lead to disqualification from the house.
